behavior_summary: U8P_CEMHAT2::hatch runs in response to an Event 7 trigger and inspects an actor's equipment to decide whether the actor possesses a dagger-like item.
At start it checks the global 'vivTold' flag and returns immediately if that flag is set (guard/early-out).

If vivTold is clear, it clears the global 'haveDagger' flag, queries the actor's equipment slot 6 via Npc::getEquip, and if an item is present it iterates (via the script's loopscr/loop mechanism) to read the item's shape.
If the item's shape equals 0x23B (inferred to be a dagger shape), the script sets global 'haveDagger' to 1.
After this check the function returns; it does not modify vivTold.
notable_control_flow: Early-return guard: read of global 'vivTold' immediately returns the function if nonzero (prevents repeated/duplicate processing).
Linear check path: clear haveDagger -> fetch equip slot -> if nonzero then run loopscr/loop to inspect item shape -> set haveDagger on match.
The item-inspection is implemented via an interpreted loop construct (loopscr/loop) rather than a direct function call; this is inferred from disassembly patterns.
open_questions: What exact gameplay event maps to 'Event 7' in this context (likely an area-enter/trigger like enterFastArea but not confirmed)?
Is shape constant 0x23B strictly the dagger item type, or a broader category that includes other items? (shape-to-item mapping is inferred, not verified).
The precise semantics of the loopscr/loop interpreter used to inspect item properties are not fully known — could affect edge cases of the check.
Does the script need to consider other equipment slots besides slot 6 for dagger detection in gameplay (currently only slot 6 is checked)?

main_function.name: U8P_CEMHAT2::hatch
main_function.purpose: On Event 7, determine whether the relevant NPC/actor possesses an equipped dagger-like item and record that fact in the global flag haveDagger, but do nothing if vivTold indicates prior processing.
entry_conditions: Triggered by Event 7 (interpreted as a fast-area/trigger event).
Receives actor pointer parameter (actor_ptr) passed on the stack (BP+06h).
Immediate guard: returns if global 'vivTold' is nonzero.

function_map.name: U8P_CEMHAT2::hatch
function_map.role: Event handler for Event 7 that checks an actor's equipment for a dagger and sets haveDagger accordingly.

function_map.name: Npc::getEquip
function_map.role: External engine function called to query the NPC's equipment in slot 6; returns an item id or 0.

external_calls.purpose: Query the actor's equipped item in slot index 6 so the script can inspect the returned item's properties (shape).

reads.name: vivTold
reads.likely_effect: Guard/early-return flag indicating this processing has already been performed; when nonzero the function exits immediately.

writes.name: haveDagger
writes.likely_effect: Cleared to 0 at start. Set to 1 if an inspected equipped item's shape equals 0x23B (records that a dagger is present).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_CEMHAT2::hatch runs in response to an Event 7 trigger and inspects an actor's equipment to decide whether the actor possesses a dagger-like item.
